implementation_summary.pairingAck_ts: New app/src/lib/pairing/pairingAck.ts. PAIRING_ACK_KIND=21060, grep-confirmed (2026-07-12) to collide with none of 444/21059/5/7/14/9/20602 (AC-SEC-3); a module-load-time assertion also fails loudly if ever edited into collision. sendPairingAck(params) builds an identity-only card via contactCard.ts#encodeCard (never encodeCardV2, so the enclosed card structurally cannot carry a pairing/nonce field, AC-ACK-1), builds the RD-6 rumor via nostr-tools/nip59#createRumor, seals+gift-wraps via directMessages.ts#sealAndWrap, and publishes via NDKEvent — malformed issuerPubkeyHex/echoedNonceHex throw (caller bugs); any other failure (signer, network, relay) is caught and reported 'queued-for-retry', never thrown. handlePairingAck(giftWrapEvent, ownPrivateKeyHex, opts) is the 9-step security-critical core: unwrap via directMessages.ts#unwrapAndOpen ONLY (never welcomeSubscription.ts's weak unwrapGiftWrap, AC-SEC-2) -> kind gate -> content shape validation -> unconditional pruneExpiredNonces sweep (AC-NONCE-6) -> isNonceAdmissible called verbatim (AC-ADMIT-2, never re-derives grace math) -> decodeCard verify -> sender-binding (decoded.pubkeyHex vs the AUTHENTICATED rumor.pubkey returned by unwrapAndOpen, never the raw gift-wrap event's outer pubkey, AC-SEC-1) -> idempotency check against a module-scope Map -> admit via rememberKnownPeers([sender]) strictly BEFORE rememberContact(sender) (ADR-005, AC-ADMIT-1), with isAllowedDmSender never imported (AC-ADMIT-4). Never throws; never constructs/sends a further ack (AC-ACK-2). getPairingAckAdmissions()/_resetPairingAckAdmissionsForTests() expose the same Map as both the AC-ACK-3 idempotency source and the admission-digest read surface S5 will consume (S3 renders no UI).
implementation_summary.welcomeSubscription_wiring: subscribeToWelcomes gained two optional trailing params (ownPrivateKeyHex, onPairingAckReceived) — fully backward compatible, existing 5-arg call sites unaffected. Inside the event handler, a new try/catch block attempts handlePairingAck before the existing (weak) unwrapGiftWrap path; 'unwrap-failed'/'wrong-kind' fall through unchanged (might still be a real Welcome/join-request), every other outcome marks the event id seen and returns early (never reaches the Welcome/join-request dispatch), firing onPairingAckReceived for admitted/already-admitted.
implementation_summary.marmotContext_wiring: startWelcomeSubscription threads the same two new params through to subscribeToWelcomes. The init() call site passes privateKeyHex ?? undefined and a callback that only logs ('[Marmot] Pairing-ack admitted from: ...') — no new React state/UI, deliberately deferring the admission-digest UI to S5.
implementation_summary.kind_isolation_AC_SEC_4: ContactChat.tsx and directMessageNotifications.ts were NOT modified — both already fail-closed on an unrecognized rumor.kind (directMessageNotifications.ts's kind1059Handler returns early when rumor.kind !== CHAT_MESSAGE_KIND). A new proof test in directMessageNotifications.test.ts exercises this existing guard with a genuine PAIRING_ACK_KIND rumor and asserts no rememberContact/incrementDirectMessage call.

tests.delta: +24 passing tests (21 pairingAck.test.ts + 2 welcomeSubscription.test.ts + 1 directMessageNotifications.test.ts). The 25 failing tests are unchanged and pre-existing (app/tests/unit/components/ThemeIcon.test.ts + app/tests/unit/themes/treatments.test.ts, an unrelated icon-set-naming mismatch from a prior theme refactor, commit 8fae18e — see baseline.json). Zero regressions.
tests.typecheck: npx tsc --noEmit -p app/tsconfig.json — clean, 0 errors, both before and after.
tests.lint: NO_LINT — no ESLint config committed in app/.

ac_coverage.AC-ACK-1: pairingAck.test.ts — sendPairingAck rumor shape, decoded via the real codec (not hand-constructed)
ac_coverage.AC-ACK-2: pairingAck.test.ts — sealAndWrap spy asserts zero calls from handlePairingAck
ac_coverage.AC-ADMIT-1: pairingAck.test.ts — ordered call-array asserts rememberKnownPeers before rememberContact
ac_coverage.AC-ADMIT-2: pairingAck.test.ts — unknown nonce + past-grace nonce, both against real isNonceAdmissible
ac_coverage.AC-ADMIT-3: pairingAck.test.ts — two distinct senders, same nonce, both admitted, nonce not consumed
ac_coverage.AC-ADMIT-4: pairingAck.ts never imports isAllowedDmSender; architecture.json dependencies_forbidden records this
ac_coverage.AC-ADMIT-5: pairingAck.test.ts — real isAllowedDmSender flips false->true post-admission
ac_coverage.AC-SEC-1: pairingAck.test.ts — harvested-card forgery fixture (real, independently-generated attacker key)
ac_coverage.AC-SEC-2: pairingAck.test.ts — tautology-check negative test; architecture.json boundary_compliance
ac_coverage.AC-SEC-3: pairingAck.test.ts + module-load-time assertion in pairingAck.ts itself
ac_coverage.AC-SEC-4: directMessageNotifications.test.ts new proof test; ContactChat.tsx/directMessageNotifications.ts confirmed unmodified
ac_coverage.AC-PRIV-1: pairingAck.test.ts — kind-0 spy + handlePairingAck's structural inability to publish (no ndk param)
ac_coverage.AC-NONCE-6 (2nd trigger): pairingAck.test.ts — unrelated stale nonce swept during ack processing

retrospective.harder_than_needed: None of the friction was in the spec — architecture.md, exploration.json, and the S2 ownership-ledger watches were unusually complete and precise (down to exact line numbers for the sender-binding primitive and the HAZARD note about welcomeSubscription's weak unwrap). The only real work was synthesizing that already-excellent research into a single coherent module design and a from-scratch attack-fixture test suite (real, independently-keyed attacker identities rather than mocked pubkey comparisons) for the sender-binding negative case, since no prior story in this epic had a security-adversarial test of this shape to pattern-match against.

absorbed_from.note: handlePairingAck's outer defense-in-depth catch-all (the 'never throws' contract) needed a fallback HandlePairingAckResult status not spelled out in the stub JSDoc; dev chose 'malformed-content' since it never risks admission and is the most plausible cause of a post-unwrap exception. Recorded as VQ-S3-023/024-adjacent follow-up territory for a future audit pass, not acted on further here since it doesn't affect any AC.

review_remediation: Parallel Opus+Codex review. Opus: sender-binding/walled-garden/kind-isolation/privacy all sound, no circumvention; 2 non-security findings fixed (loud empty-name guard in sendPairingAck; malformed 'unknown nonce' test assertion). Codex caught a cross-cutting regression Opus missed: v1->v2 share switch broke NIP-46 bunker signing (REQUIRED_PERMS lacked sign_event:20602) — fixed in NostrIdentityContext.tsx. NIP-46 perm list has no unit coverage (React module, no-jsdom) — documented gap in ledger watches. Examiner 24/24 YES.
